titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L425
GA L425
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Verse marker K1V33 moved after επ αυτον by David Parker.
K13V3 at S5W1D5b changed to K13V1 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 2.4 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 19.10-2 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word element at 11.22 fixed by Rachel Kevern.
Ghost pages removed by Catherine Smith.
Word breaks at 16.10 (second occurrence) and 16.21 (second occurrence) corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word breaks at 11.22 and 12.48 corrected by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7b Status:G-A number: L 425 (Athens, Bib. Νat., 59)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1r(F 4r has a few words in Arabic)Τranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 03.07.02Τranscription finished: 05.11.02Τransference to Collate by: 03.07.02Τransference to Collate begun: RLMΤransference to Collate finished: 05.11.02File date and time:Ρlaced in Level1: RLMLevel1 file date and time: 26.10.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Karavidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Menologion read in MuensterΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 15.11.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 28.10.13 End date: 1.11.13Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes. Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No. Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No. 7.53 - 8.11 No. Other remarks: Unidentified reading 1 is on F218v: John 3,13-17.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
The beginning of this lection (13.31ff) is lacunose.

ab.note: Heading added in the bottom margin: του αγιου μωκιου.

div.note: John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
This lection has no heading and follows straight on from Matthew.
The heading instructs the user to read the lection on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.

ab.note: These are instructions to locate the reading for M1 D14a: John 12, 28-36.
Gospel Resurrection 9 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross-referenced to the S1 W2 D1 reading.
